resolvedQuestions.text: Which canonical representation and adapters should v1 use?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: Adapter pattern with format-specific parsers (MDAST/Markdown, HAST/HTML, mammoth/DOCX) normalizing to canonical text representation. Format tiers: Full (Text/Markdown/HTML), Limited (DOCX), Unsupported (PDF).

resolvedQuestions.text: Which diff algorithms best suppress conversion noise?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: jsdiff (Myers O(ND)) with format-specific normalizers. Key noise-suppression tactics: token-level granularity, whitespace normalization, canonical re-serialization via AST, metadata exclusion.

resolvedQuestions.text: Which snapshot lifecycle is safe and predictable?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: Four-phase model (Detect->Capture->Compare->Cleanup). Filesystem storage under ~/.document-snapshots/. SHA-256 hash detection. TTL-based expiration (7d) + pair-based retention (10 per file). Explicit API primary; optional chokidar watcher.

resolvedQuestions.text: How should the HTML report expose fidelity and risk?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: diff2html with fidelity header (format match, conversion fidelity, change severity, risk level). Self-contained single HTML file. No external dependencies or network requests.

resolvedQuestions.text: Which portable core and skill interface should v1 implement?
resolvedQuestions.resolution: TypeScript/Node.js core (document-diff-core) with 5 CLI commands + programmatic API. OpenCode skill wraps core with workspace-aware path resolution. npm packaging with optional DOCX dependency.

keyFindings.text: jsdiff v9.0.0 is the dominant JS diff library (95M weekly downloads, BSD-3-Clause)
diff2html provides production-grade HTML diff rendering with side-by-side, syntax highlighting, and self-contained output
MDAST/HAST (syntax-tree ecosystem) provides standardized AST representations for Markdown and HTML
diff-match-patch is archived (Aug 2024) and not viable as a v1 dependency
Format-specific normalization before diffing is key to suppressing conversion noise
mammoth v1.12.0 converts DOCX to HTML with semantic style mapping (5.2M weekly downloads)
PDF structural comparison is infeasible in v1 — correctly deferred
Filesystem-based snapshots with SHA-256 hashes are the simplest v1 storage approach
Node.js/TypeScript is the clear runtime choice — all dependencies are npm-native
Mammoth output must be sanitized (HAST pipeline) due to XSS risk from DOCX sources
CLI-first design with 5 commands provides both human and AI agent accessibility
All 5 research charter questions answered with evidence-backed recommendations

ruledOutDirections.reason: No diff2html equivalent; HTML rendering gap
